Hero.subtitle: Foundation Year · German Language · University Pathway

About.title: 20 Years of Excellence. Now in Europe.
About.description1: Founded in 2005, Target International School has grown from a single classroom in Tashkent into one of Central Asia's most respected and trusted educational institutions. Today, with 5 campuses, over 12,000 successful graduates, and alumni accepted into leading universities across the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, South Korea, and Europe - including TU Munich, Heidelberg, and Humboldt - we have built a reputation for academic excellence that delivers measurable results.
About.description2: Now, we are bringing that same proven methodology to Germany. Our new European campus offers students an unmatched opportunity: immerse yourself in one of the world's finest education systems, learn German from certified native-speaking instructors using CEFR-aligned curricula, prepare for the Studienkolleg (Feststellungspruefung), and progress directly into tuition-free German public universities. With full visa support, student housing assistance, and career counseling, your journey from Tashkent to Berlin is fully supported.
About.missionText: To bridge Central Asia and Europe through world-class education by empowering every student with German language proficiency, rigorous academic preparation, and a clear pathway to tuition-free university education at Germany's top institutions. We believe every talented student deserves access to world-class education regardless of financial background.
About.visionText: To become the leading international educational gateway for students seeking affordable, globally recognized higher education in Germany by graduating the next generation of engineers, doctors, business leaders, and tech innovators who will shape the future of both Central Asia and Europe.
About.whyGermanySubtitle: More than 400,000 international students already chose Germany. Here is why you should too.
About.reason1Desc: Unlike the US (USD 50,000+/year) or UK (GBP 15,000+/year), German public universities charge zero tuition fees for international students. You only pay a small semester contribution of about EUR 150-EUR 350, which often includes a public transport pass for your city.
About.reason2Desc: Germany is home to 46 universities ranked in the global top 500, including TU Munich, LMU Munich, Heidelberg, RWTH Aachen, Charite Berlin, and Humboldt University. German degrees are respected by employers worldwide.
About.reason3Desc: After graduation, Germany grants an 18-month job-seeking visa to international graduates. Once employed, you can transition to an EU Blue Card, offering a fast track to permanent residency for high-demand professions like IT, engineering, and medicine.
About.reason4Desc: Germany ranks among the safest countries in the world. With more than 400,000 international students from 190+ countries, you join a vibrant global community in the center of Europe with easy access to the wider Schengen region.
About.reason5Desc: Germany is the world's third-largest exporter and home to industry leaders like BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Siemens, SAP, Bosch, BASF, and Bayer. STEM graduates face very low unemployment, and IT professionals often start above EUR 52,000 per year.
About.reason6Desc: Average monthly living costs range from EUR 700-EUR 950 depending on the city. That typically covers shared accommodation or student housing, health insurance, food, and transport, with additional student discounts across museums, culture, and recreation.

Comparison.title: Why Choose Target International
Comparison.description: We do not just process applications; we build careers. Here is how our comprehensive approach compares to traditional agencies and language schools.
Comparison.feature1Others: Application assistance only with no academic pathway

Comparison.feature4Target: Dedicated career support and corporate partnerships
Comparison.feature5Target: Purpose-built European flagship campus in Germany

Guidelines.title: Your Roadmap to Success in Germany
Guidelines.description: Navigating a new country can be overwhelming. We provide structured guidance to help you thrive academically and professionally in Germany.
Guidelines.step1Desc: A full checklist covering visa requirements, blocked account setup (EUR 11,904), and essential documentation.
Guidelines.step2Desc: Learn the German higher education system, understand ECTS credits, and adapt to effective study methods for international students.
Guidelines.step3Desc: International students can work up to 140 full days or 280 half days per year. We help you find part-time roles that match your studies.
Guidelines.step4Desc: Guidance on the 18-month job-seeker visa, moving to an EU Blue Card, and building a long-term future in Europe.

Programs.title: Your Pathway, Your Choice
Programs.description: Whether you want to enter a German university through Studienkolleg, master the German language from scratch, prepare for international standardized tests, or build a career in business and technology, we have a structured program designed for your goals.
Programs.program1Desc: The Studienkolleg is a one-year university preparatory program recognized by German public universities. Students complete intensive academic preparation in their chosen track, take the Feststellungspruefung (FSP), and progress toward direct admission to universities such as TU Munich, LMU, Heidelberg, and RWTH Aachen.
Programs.program1Feature2: Intensive German language training to B2 or C1 within 12 months
Programs.program1Feature3: Subject tracks: T-Kurs (Engineering and Tech), W-Kurs (Business and Economics), M-Kurs (Medicine and Biology)
Programs.program1Feature4: University placement support with 40+ partner universities
Programs.program2Desc: Our German Language Academy offers intensive and super-intensive courses following the CEFR framework. Whether you are a beginner or preparing for university fluency, you can reach your target level with accelerated instruction from certified native-speaking teachers.
Programs.program2Feature1: All CEFR levels covered: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2
Programs.program2Feature2: TestDaF, DSH, telc, and Goethe-Zertifikat preparation included
Programs.program2Feature3: Small classes of up to 15 students for individual attention
Programs.program2Feature4: Certified native German instructors with 5+ years of teaching experience
Programs.program3Desc: As a certified College Board SAT Test Center and accredited AP tutor, Target International has a proven record of producing strong standardized test scores. Our instructors use data-driven methods and real exam practice to keep students ahead of national averages.
Programs.program3Feature1: Official College Board SAT Test Center (Center Code: 694851)
Programs.program3Feature2: Accredited AP courses across 15+ subjects including Calculus, Physics, and Computer Science
Programs.program3Feature3: IELTS preparation targeting Band 7.0+ with Cambridge-certified instructors
Programs.program3Feature4: One-on-one university application counseling, statement review, and interview preparation
Programs.program4Desc: A specialized route for students aiming at Germany's growing tech and business sectors. Study practical business administration, finance, data science, and software engineering through project-based learning, industry mentors, and internship opportunities.
Programs.program4Feature1: Project-based learning with German industry case studies
Programs.program4Feature2: Industry partnerships and internship access at German firms
Programs.program4Feature3: Entrepreneurship workshops and startup incubator support
Programs.program4Feature4: Modern tech lab with current hardware, cloud resources, and digital tools

German.title: Master German. Unlock Germany.
German.description: Our German Language Academy delivers structured, CEFR-aligned courses taught by certified native German speakers. Whether you are starting from zero or preparing for university admission exams like TestDaF or DSH, we guide you to university-ready fluency with a proven methodology and a 92% exam pass rate.
German.levelA1Desc: Learn basic greetings, introductions, numbers, and everyday phrases. Build your foundation in German grammar, pronunciation, and writing.
German.levelA2Desc: Handle routine conversations about family, work, and daily life. Describe your surroundings, express needs, and navigate life in German-speaking countries with confidence.
German.levelB1Desc: Express opinions, discuss current events, manage travel and workplace situations, and write structured texts on familiar topics.
German.levelB2Desc: Interact fluently with native speakers on complex topics. Understand academic lectures, write formal emails and reports, and join professional discussions.
German.levelC1Desc: Express ideas fluently and precisely on academic and professional topics. C1 is the standard requirement for many German university admissions.
German.levelC2Desc: Achieve near-native fluency. Understand virtually everything you hear or read and reconstruct complex arguments with precision.
German.intensiveDesc: 20 hours/week - 8 weeks per level. A balanced format combining grammar, conversation practice, and exam preparation.
German.superIntensiveDesc: 30 hours/week - 6 weeks per level. Includes daily immersion exercises, one-on-one tutoring, and mock exam practice.
German.eveningDesc: 8 hours/week - 14 weeks per level. Designed for working professionals and part-time learners with flexible scheduling.
German.examPrepDesc: Specialized preparation to help you achieve the language certification required by your target German university, including reading, listening, writing, and speaking practice with weekly mock exams.

Opportunities.title: Where a German Degree Takes You
Opportunities.description: A degree from a German university does not just open doors - it removes walls. Germany's education system ranks among the strongest for employability, and graduates enjoy strong starting salaries and low unemployment rates.
Opportunities.uni1Desc: Germany's top-ranked university and a global leader in engineering, natural sciences, computer science, and technology.
Opportunities.uni2Desc: One of Europe's most prestigious research universities with excellence in law, medicine, humanities, and natural sciences.
Opportunities.uni3Desc: Germany's oldest university and one of the world's most distinguished institutions, renowned for medicine, molecular biology, physics, and philosophy.
Opportunities.uni4Desc: Home to 57 Nobel Prize winners and a landmark institution in the heart of Berlin.
Opportunities.uni5Desc: Europe's leading technical university and a top destination for mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, and technology research.
Opportunities.uni6Desc: A leading research university known for humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and international academic exchange.
Opportunities.pathway2: EU Blue Card eligibility for skilled professionals earning EUR 45,300+/year
Opportunities.pathway4: Very low unemployment for STEM, IT, and engineering graduates
Opportunities.pathway5: Permanent residency eligibility after sustained employment
Opportunities.pathway6: Direct access to major employers headquartered in Germany

Campus.title: From Tashkent to Berlin
Campus.description: 6 campuses. 2 countries. 1 mission - to give every ambitious student access to a world-class education and a direct pathway to Europe's finest universities.
Campus.germanyDesc: Our European flagship campus is a modern learning center in Germany designed for international students preparing for university admission. It features advanced classrooms, a dedicated language lab, multimedia lecture halls, and collaborative study spaces in a well-connected urban location.
Campus.germanyFeature1: Smart classrooms with interactive digital boards
Campus.germanyFeature2: Dedicated German language laboratory with audio-visual resources
Campus.germanyFeature3: Student lounge, co-working spaces, and quiet study rooms
Campus.germanyFeature4: Central location with S-Bahn and U-Bahn access
Campus.tashkentDesc: 5 established campuses across Tashkent, each specializing in different academic disciplines and serving over 2,400 students per year.
Campus.qoraqamishDesc: STEM Innovation Hub - Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics
Campus.tinchlikDesc: Business, IT, and Computer Science Center
Campus.yunusabadDesc: SAT, IELTS, and University Prep Hub
Campus.sergeliDesc: Athletics, Sports, and Student Activities Complex

Admissions.title: Your Journey Starts Here
Admissions.description: Applying to Target International is simple and straightforward. Our admissions team guides you from the first application to your first day on campus in Germany, and most students receive a decision within 5 business days.
Admissions.step1Desc: Complete the online form with your academic transcripts, passport copy, and a short personal statement about your educational goals and why you want to study in Germany.
Admissions.step2Desc: Our academic evaluation team reviews your application within 3-5 business days. Qualified candidates are invited to a 20-minute interview online or in person.
Admissions.step3Desc: Receive your official acceptance letter and enrollment confirmation. Our visa team guides you through the entire German student visa process, including blocked account setup (EUR 11,904), health insurance, and embassy preparation.
Admissions.step4Desc: Arrive at campus, attend orientation, complete city registration and banking, and begin your academic life in Germany with full support.
Admissions.formDesc: Fill out the form below and our admissions team will contact you within 24 hours with the next steps.
Admissions.success: Application submitted successfully. Our admissions team will contact you within 24 hours.

Footer.aboutText: Bridging Central Asia and Europe through world-class education since 2005. With 5 established campuses in Tashkent and our new flagship campus in Germany, Target International School provides a complete pathway from German language mastery to tuition-free university admission.
Footer.rights: All rights reserved. Target International School.
